16-May
1/  The end-of-month data in this table include foreign holdings of U.S. Treasury marketable and non-marketable bills, bonds,
and notes reported monthly under the Treasury International Capital (TIC) reporting system. The data are collected
primarily from U.S.-based custodians of securities. Since U.S. securities held in overseas custody accounts may not be
attributed to the actual owners, the data may not provide a precise accounting of individual country ownership of Treasury
securities (see TIC FAQ no. 7 at:  http://www.treasury.gov/resource-center/data-chart-center/tic/Pages/ticfaq1.aspx).
(a)  Data for September 2011 and for all months beginning December 2011 include holdings of U.S. Treasury bonds and
notes as reported on TIC Form SLT, "Aggregate Holdings of Long-Term Securities by U.S. and Foreign Residents".
(b) Data before December 2011 were collected primarily from U.S.-based custodians and broker-dealers.
-- Data for December of 1974, 1978, 1984, 1989, 1994, and March 2000 were reported on the TIC Benchmark Survey of Foreign
Holdings of U.S. Securities for those dates.  Data for each June in the period 2002 - 2011 were reported on the TIC Annual
Survey of Foreign Holdings of U.S. Securities for those ten dates.
-- Data for all other months before December 2011 include estimated foreign holdings of U.S. Treasury bonds and notes
based on adding monthly net transactions in Treasury bonds/notes from the TIC Form S, "Purchases and Sales of Long-Term
Securities by Foreign-Residents", to the holdings in the most recent TIC Benchmark or Annual Survey of Foreign Holdings
of U.S. Securities.  The data for October and November of 2011 include estimated foreign holdings of U.S. Treasury bonds
and notes based on the September 2011 SLT data and monthly net transactions data.
